reasoning: The 10-year TIPS yield has declined significantly from around 2.16% in May 2025 to 1.73% by late October 2025. However, since mid-September, the yield has relatively stabilized, oscillating within a range of roughly 1.68% to 1.84%. The most recent value as of the October 26 cutoff is 1.73%, which is near the lower boundary of this recent range. The statistical analysis of the FRED tool provides a primary probability anchor of 0.63, combining a linear mean-reversion estimate with seasonal historical trends that favorably point to an incremental increase in the near term. Applying this, the probability for the first proximate resolution date (2025-11-02) is set at 0.63. Over longer horizons, forecasting interest rates becomes substantially more difficult, especially with possible macroeconomic policy shifts or rate cuts. As per both statistical logic and explicit guidelines, the uncertainty must increase for distant resolution dates, meaning probabilities should gravitate closer to 0.5. Therefore, the estimates for the 1-month and 3-month horizons are correspondingly moderated to 0.58 and 0.53.
